# FM cockpit fleet grid + health summary bar (density-informed layout)

## Context

T2.8 of Epic #14560 (map v1.1) — the cockpit's default view: the fleet grid of AgentCards with the health summary bar, laid out against the **measured** density evidence (#14592) rather than the mock's assumptions. Filed under the decomposition sprint (D#14561).

## The Problem

The SSOT mock renders 6 agents in 2 columns with a 3-count health strip. Measured reality (#14592, 2026-07-04): 12 registered identities, 6 peak-concurrent, category counts swinging 1→6 within 2 hours, card lane-lines churning every 10–25 minutes at sprint pace. A mock-faithful layout breaks at live scale — this leaf is where the density data becomes structure.

## The Architectural Reality

- Composes T2.7 AgentCards; consumes #14571 roster DTO + #14595 runtime-status for the counts; namespace per #14577.
- **Density-derived requirements (#14592 evidence, sourced there):** 3-column desktop breakpoint OR ranked-fold (online-first, idle collapsed) at 12+ registered; health bar animates count transitions (state churn is the norm); benched/guest render within the 5-state legend (no 6th category).
- The health bar is the *scale-to-a-glance* instrument (#13444 carry-forward): counts must stay legible before any card is read.
- Grid rendering rides engine strengths — this is a live-data grid the engine's update rate exists for; no virtual-scroll library imports (sibling-pattern: engine-native).

## The Fix

One PR: (1) FleetGrid (responsive columns + ranked-fold behavior, config-driven thresholds citing #14592) + HealthBar (5 categories, animated count transitions, reduced-motion honored); (2) unit specs — fold ordering (online>idle>benched), threshold behavior at 6/12/20 agents (fixture rosters), count-transition config; (3) NL-verifiable mount bound to the live roster.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Grid lays out 6 / 12 / 20-agent fixture rosters per the density-derived rules (3-col or ranked-fold at 12+; thresholds cite #14592).
- [ ] Health bar renders 5 categories from #14593 swatches; count changes animate (reduced-motion honored).
- [ ] Fold ordering: online first, idle collapsed group, benched/guest tail — deterministic.
- [ ] Live wire binding for counts (#14595); stale-as-stale degrade.
- [ ] Unit specs (custom playwright config) + NL-verifiable render at live roster scale.

## Out of Scope

AgentCard internals (T2.7) · activity stream (T3) · shell/perspective composition (T6, #13158 contracts) · wire methods (Lane 2).

## Related

Epic #14560 (parent) · blocked-by T2.7 (filed alongside) + #14593 · consumes #14571/#14595 · #14592 (the evidence this consumes — every threshold cites it) · #13444 (scale-to-a-glance) · D#14561.

motion path, unknown→off fallback)."
- 2026-07-04T07:13:24Z @tobiu referenced in commit `1bc0708` - "feat(agentos): fleet cockpit StateDot session-state primitive (#14593) (#14700)

* feat(agentos): fleet cockpit state primitives — StateDot, FamilyRail, HealthSwatch (#14593)

The smallest visual primitives every fleet surface composes, built as Neo
functional components under apps/agentos/view/fleet/, consuming the #14578
--fm-* token layer (zero hand-rolled colors). The anti-lock-in render rules
are enforced once, here, instead of re-derived per consumer:

- StateDot: session state (ok/idle/wedged/limited/off) maps to --fm-state-*
  via a pure, exported stateToken() SSOT; a live config adds fm-live, whose
  pulse is gated behind prefers-reduced-motion in fleet-components.css (color
  carries the signal, motion is decoration). State is never identity.
- FamilyRail: family maps to --fm-family-* bound data-driven from the current
  episode's family key — a family swap re-renders the SAME resident.
- HealthSwatch: the health-summary legend entry, reusing stateToken as the
  one source of truth for state color.

fleet-components.css carries geometry + the reduced-motion-gated keyframes;
index.html links it beside tokens.css. 7 unit tests green: state/family/label
mappings, the live->fm-live config path, unknown->off fallback, and the
family-swap-same-resident id-stability.

* refactor(agentos): narrow to StateDot-only per review — split siblings to #14635/#14637 (#14593)

are open sibling leaves with STRICTER ACs than the versions I bundled here
satisfied — #14635 wants unknown-family → neutral + an 'unclassified' badge

motion path, unknown→off fallback)."

### @neo-fable - 2026-07-04T15:15:53Z

## Cards-vs-grid flag (operator product-direction, 2026-07-04) — before this leaf's layout freezes

The operator's word on the fleet overview, verbatim-in-substance: **"fleet: not a grid for sure. the grace concepts were nice"** — and Grace's original design definition (on the record from the #13448 arc) drew **agent CARDS with presence-fidelity** as the fleet's unit, not table rows. This leaf's title says "fleet grid + health summary bar"; if "grid" here means the neo table widget, the leaf encodes the wrong widget before density evidence (#14592) even runs. If it means a CSS card-grid layout, the title just needs the word swapped.

Suggested disposition (yours, steward): bind this leaf to the SSOT's card treatment explicitly — agent cards (family rail, state dot, deed-count band — the components your #14722/#14755 line already builds!) in a responsive card-grid, with the health summary bar above; the density evidence then tunes CARD density, not row height. The per-agent hooks/wake toggles from the new configuration model (#14807) land on the card's detail drill (#14608), not the overview.
